name: Sol
summary: The Sun (Sol) is a G2V main-sequence star at the center of the Solar System, containing about 99.86% of its total mass. With a diameter of approximately 1.39 million kilometers and a surface temperature around 5,500°C, it emits the energy that drives Earth’s climate and makes life possible. Energy is produced through nuclear fusion in its core, where temperatures reach roughly 15 million degrees Celsius. Formed about 4.6 billion years ago, the Sun is currently in a stable phase but will eventually evolve into a red giant in about 5 billion years.
